login.q: Can users deploy without logging in?
login.a: No. Repository validation and deploy endpoints require a valid user JWT and protected frontend route.

subdomains.a: Point wildcard DNS to the Linux server and use the provided Nginx or Caddy config.

postgres.a: Yes. Production uses the included PostgreSQL migration. Local development can use the file fallback.

https.a: The app includes Caddy and Nginx plus Certbot deployment guides for wildcard or domain HTTPS.

frameworks.q: Can I deploy React or Vue?
frameworks.a: Yes. DreamX detects package files, runs the build command, and publishes the output directory.

privateRepos.a: Yes. Connect GitHub or GitLab with OAuth; DreamX stores access tokens encrypted on the server.

limits.a: Yes. Project limits, build timeout, maintenance mode, branding, and SMTP can be changed from settings.

logs.a: Deployment and admin logs are persisted and can also stream live over WebSocket.

domains.a: The deployment model supports clean slugs and wildcard subdomains, with reverse proxy examples included.

dashboard.title: Your deployment workspace.
dashboard.copy: Three active slots are shown by default. Delete or stop a project to free a slot.
dashboard.workspaceCopy: Deploy slots, activity, usage, and onboarding live in one calm workspace.

deploy.title: Connect a source, analyze, then publish.
deploy.copy: Only authenticated users can deploy. GitHub and GitLab repositories are validated before clone, build, and publish.
